whoa wtf, "Yarvin's opinions have been described as racist, with his writings interpreted as supportive of slavery, including the belief that whites have higher IQs than blacks for genetic reasons. Yarvin himself maintains that he is a racist because he doubts that "all races are equally smart," and supports the notion "that people who score higher on IQ tests are in some sense superior human beings". He also describes himself as being an "outspoken advocate for slavery",[8][22] and has argued that some races are more suited to slavery than others.[23]"  That's a level past standard alt-right racism.  Return to slavery justified by IQ tests?

text: Nay — different audience, different goals. But I do support the idea of "user liberation". I intend that anyone should be _able_ to use the stuff I'm building, but most people wouldn't want or need to, since it solves a very particular problem having to do with communication media.

Nay — different audience, different goals. But I do support the idea of "user liberation". I intend that anyone should be _able_ to use the stuff I'm building, but most people wouldn't want or need to, since it solves a very particular problem having to do with communication media.

The word “activist” seems to require buying into a specific solution that you then go out to evangelize in the world.  I’m still in the research phase along 3 fronts:
• Trying to come up with a solution.
• Tracking the solutions others come up with from similar goals.
• Figuring out the right ways to evangelize a solution once I have it.

definitely in favor of user liberation here! I’ve also been using the related label “personal computation” (as in <https://twitter.com/maxkreminski/status/1121174498533732352>) for some of my own work in this direction

attachments.fallback:
<https://twitter.com/maxkreminski|@maxkreminski>: officially planting a flag in the ground for “personal computation” as a research area involving the reclaiming of computation for individuals & small communities

overlaps with end-user programming, humane digital social platforms, player creativity in games, anarchist AI, etc

The slavery stuff is more concerning on a practical level, but it doesn’t even fully capture how extreme their beliefs are given that people have expressed the same position in  mainstream (regional?) politics relatively recently in history. My mind was blown when I realized they’re straight up _monarchists_. I haven’t looked into it but I haven’t heard of Americans advocating for having a king rule the country since right after the revolution. (Yes, a king specifically.)
